content:
First observe that

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
=abc+(a+b+c)+\Bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c\Bigr)+\frac1{abc}.
\]

Hence it is an integer if and only if
\[
S\;=\;\frac1a+\frac1b+\frac1c+\frac1{abc}
\]
is an integer.  Write
\[
S=\frac{bc+ca+ab+1}{abc}\;=\;k\quad(k\in\Bbb Z).
\]
Since \(a,b,c>0\), one checks easily that the only possible integer values of \(k\) and corresponding triples \((a,b,c)\) (up to permutation) are

\begin{itemize}
\item \(k=4\):   \((1,1,1)\).
\item \(k=3\):   \((1,1,2)\).
\item \(k=2\):   \((1,2,3)\).
\item \(k=1\):   \((2,3,7)\).
\end{itemize}

Counting ordered permutations,

\[
\begin{aligned}
&(1,1,1)\quad\text{gives }1,\\
&(1,1,2)\quad\text{gives }3,\\
&(1,2,3)\quad\text{gives }6,\\
&(2,3,7)\quad\text{gives }6,
\end{aligned}
\]
for a total of \(1+3+6+6=16\). 

Therefore the number of ordered triples \((a,b,c)\) is 
\[
\boxed{16}.
\]

message.content:
First observe that

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
=abc+(a+b+c)+\Bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c\Bigr)+\frac1{abc}.
\]

Hence it is an integer if and only if
\[
S\;=\;\frac1a+\frac1b+\frac1c+\frac1{abc}
\]
is an integer.  Write
\[
S=\frac{bc+ca+ab+1}{abc}\;=\;k\quad(k\in\Bbb Z).
\]
Since \(a,b,c>0\), one checks easily that the only possible integer values of \(k\) and corresponding triples \((a,b,c)\) (up to permutation) are

\begin{itemize}
\item \(k=4\):   \((1,1,1)\).
\item \(k=3\):   \((1,1,2)\).
\item \(k=2\):   \((1,2,3)\).
\item \(k=1\):   \((2,3,7)\).
\end{itemize}

Counting ordered permutations,

\[
\begin{aligned}
&(1,1,1)\quad\text{gives }1,\\
&(1,1,2)\quad\text{gives }3,\\
&(1,2,3)\quad\text{gives }6,\\
&(2,3,7)\quad\text{gives }6,
\end{aligned}
\]
for a total of \(1+3+6+6=16\). 

Therefore the number of ordered triples \((a,b,c)\) is 
\[
\boxed{16}.
\]
